title: Task 3: Assistant context lifecycle APIs
description:
Implements Task 3 from `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md`.

## Required Reading
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Task 3

## Scope
Implement the public `ExClaw.Assistant` context APIs that later UI and runner code will call.
- Add scope-aware session APIs for listing, loading, creating, renaming, archiving, and updating `current_model`.
- Implement latest-session lookup and default-session creation using the configured default backend and the configured default model for that backend.
- Implement user-message creation plus run lifecycle helpers for start, complete, fail, and event recording.
- Preserve the approved invariants: `last_message_at` tracks transcript activity only, retries reuse the original user message while creating a new run, and assistant messages are only created for successful runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Public context functions exist for the lifecycle APIs described in the plan.
- [ ] Context tests verify latest-session behavior, archive filtering, `current_model` updates, run lifecycle transitions, and `last_message_at` semantics.
- [ ] Test verification passes: `mix test test/ex_claw/assistant_test.exs`
- [ ] After completion, summarize changes and verification, ask the user to review/commit, and do not self-commit.

title: Task 2: Assistant persistence migration, schemas, and fixtures
description:
Implements Task 2 from `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md`.

## Required Reading
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md` section `Schema semantics and invariants`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Task 2

## Scope
Add the assistant persistence layer that matches the approved schema contract.
- Generate and implement the migration for `assistant_sessions`, `assistant_messages`, `assistant_runs`, and `assistant_run_events`.
- Add indexes and constraints, including the unique partial constraint that enforces at most one assistant message per run.
- Implement Ecto schemas/changesets aligned with the approved invariants for ownership, roles, statuses, timestamps, archived access, and append-only versus mutable tables.
- Add assistant fixtures that support later context, runner, and LiveView tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Focused tests cover session ownership, archive semantics, message/run invariants, and run-event ordering.
- [ ] The migration and schemas match the documented field meanings and nullability rules.
- [ ] Fixtures exist for sessions, messages, runs, and run events.
- [ ] Test verification passes: `mix test test/ex_claw/assistant_test.exs`
- [ ] After completion, summarize changes and verification, ask the user to review/commit, and do not self-commit.

title: Task 1: Backend contract, defaults, model catalog, and fake backend scaffold
description:
Implements Task 1 from `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md`.

## Required Reading
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Task 1

## Scope
Create the shared backend contract and configuration foundation used by the rest of the slice.
- Add assistant config with `default_backend: :auggie` and per-backend defaults, with `:auggie` initially using default model `gpt5.4`.
- Treat runtime settings as deployment config only, not user-editable preferences.
- Define `ExClaw.Assistant.Backend` with explicit `list_models/0` and `run_turn/1` contracts.
- Make `list_models/0` return normalized model info and `run_turn/1` use the approved normalized request/result shapes.
- Implement `ExClaw.Assistant.Backends` registry helpers and `ExClaw.Assistant.ModelCatalog` with resilient startup and per-backend error state.
- Start the model catalog and `ExClaw.Assistant.TaskSupervisor` in the application.
- Add a deterministic fake backend for tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Focused red/green tests cover backend registry lookup, model discovery, model-catalog availability/error state, and fake-backend swapping.
- [ ] The backend contract includes the approved `list_models/0` and `run_turn/1` request/result semantics, including normalized message shape, backend-session continuation lookup, and tagged success/failure results.
- [ ] Model catalog startup does not crash the app when model discovery fails.
- [ ] Test verification passes: `mix test test/ex_claw/assistant/backends/auggie_test.exs`
- [ ] After completion, summarize changes and verification, ask the user to review/commit, and do not self-commit.

title: Phase 1: Foundation contracts and persistence
description:
Implements the foundation portion of `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` (Tasks 1-3).

## Context
This phase creates the backend contract, model catalog, fake backend, persistence schema, fixtures, and Assistant context lifecycle APIs. All later UI and real-backend work depends on these contracts being stable.

## Required Reading
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Tasks 1-3

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Backend contracts, model discovery, and fake backend scaffolding are in place.
- [ ] Assistant persistence schema and fixtures match the documented invariants.
- [ ] Assistant context lifecycle APIs exist for sessions, messages, runs, and run events.

title: Assistant Slice 1
description:
Execute the approved Assistant Slice 1 implementation plan.

## Context
- Source documents:
  - `docs/prd/2026-04-05-assistant-slice-1.md`
  - `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
  - `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md`
- This work must follow the dependency-ordered implementation plan rather than a 1:1 PRD-story order.
- The slice includes: authenticated assistant routes, root redirect, persisted sessions/messages/runs/events, session management UI, per-session model selection, async run orchestration, inline debug detail, and a real `auggie` backend behind a shared abstraction.

## Execution Rules
- Preserve the documented contracts and schema semantics from the design doc.
- Use the fake backend in tests until the real `auggie` adapter bead.
- Do not self-commit. After each execution bead, summarize changes and verification, ask the user to review/commit, and wait for approval.
- Only update docs during execution when clarifying technical reality or correcting assumptions; do not silently change approved scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The child beads execute the approved implementation plan in dependency order.
- [ ] The resulting work remains traceable back to the PRD, design doc, and implementation plan.
- [ ] Final integration bead runs `mix test`, `mix precommit`, and the required browser sanity checks.

title: Task 5: Session list UI, metadata, model selection, and archive UX
description:
Implements Task 5 from `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md`.

## Required Reading
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Task 5

## Scope
Implement the session-management UX on top of the `SessionsLive` shell.
- Show session list items with title, backend, current model, created timestamp, and last active timestamp.
- Support create, switch, rename, and archive actions.
- Populate the per-session model selector from the shared model catalog.
- Allow model changes during an active run, but apply them only to future runs.
- Keep an archived current session on screen in read-only mode with the message `This session is archived.`
- Continue using stream-based collection updates and stable test IDs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LiveView tests cover session metadata rendering, create/switch/rename/archive, archived-current-session read-only behavior, model selection, and model changes during active runs.
- [ ] Session list updates use stream operations rather than plain list assigns.
- [ ] Focused verification passes: `mix test test/ex_claw_web/live/sessions_live_test.exs`
- [ ] Perform a lightweight browser sanity check for session management and archived-session UX.
- [ ] After completion, summarize changes and verification, ask the user to review/commit, and do not self-commit.

title: Task 4: Authenticated routes, namespace redirect, and sessions LiveView shell
description:
Implements Task 4 from `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md`.

## Required Reading
- `docs/prd/2026-04-05-assistant-slice-1.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Task 4

## Scope
Add the authenticated assistant entry points and minimal sessions shell.
- Place the assistant namespace routes in the existing authenticated scope and the session UI routes in the existing `live_session :require_authenticated_user`.
- Redirect authenticated `/` and `signed_in_path/1` to `/assistant`.
- Implement `/assistant` as the assistant namespace root that redirects to `/assistant/sessions`.
- Implement `/assistant/sessions` so it loads the current user's latest non-archived session or creates one, then navigates to `/assistant/sessions/:session_id`.
- Implement `/assistant/sessions/:session_id` so it loads the requested session when it belongs to the current user, redirects missing/inaccessible sessions to `/assistant/sessions` with a flash, and renders archived sessions read-only with the message `This session is archived.`
- Build the sessions shell with `<Layouts.app flash={@flash} current_scope={@current_scope}>`, stable DOM IDs, and LiveView streams for the session list and transcript.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Controller/auth/live tests cover unauthenticated `/`, authenticated root redirect, signed-in redirects, `/assistant` redirect behavior, `/assistant/sessions` landing behavior, and `/assistant/sessions/:session_id` access rules.
- [ ] The shell uses streams for the session list and transcript, with non-collection UI state kept in normal assigns.
- [ ] Archived sessions remain viewable in read-only mode and show `This session is archived.` near the disabled composer.
- [ ] Focused verification passes:
- [ ] `mix test test/ex_claw_web/controllers/assistant_controller_test.exs`
- [ ] `mix test test/ex_claw_web/controllers/page_controller_test.exs`
- [ ] `mix test test/ex_claw_web/user_auth_test.exs`
- [ ] `mix test test/ex_claw_web/live/sessions_live_test.exs`
- [ ] Perform a lightweight browser sanity check for the new routing/shell behavior.
- [ ] After completion, summarize changes and verification, ask the user to review/commit, and do not self-commit.

title: Phase 2: Authenticated shell and session UX
description:
Implements the UI shell and session-management portion of `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` (Tasks 4-5).

## Context
This phase adds authenticated routes, redirects, the assistant LiveView shell, session list UX, per-session model selection, and archived-session read-only behavior. It must build on the Phase 1 contracts rather than inventing new shapes.

## Required Reading
- `docs/prd/2026-04-05-assistant-slice-1.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Tasks 4-5

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Authenticated users land in the assistant workspace via `/assistant`.
- [ ] Session list and transcript shell follow the documented route, archive, and stream behavior.
- [ ] Session metadata, model selection, and read-only archived-session UX are implemented.

title: Define inline interaction wording in docs
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: clarify the architecture docs so 'inline' is explicitly defined as work handled within the current user interaction, not delegated to an independent persisted background task.

title: Restore hybrid/reactive interaction model wording
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: update the architecture docs so the interaction model is described as hybrid in architecture but reactive in behavior, which better matches the chosen LiveView-first design with a planned HTTP API sharing lower-level logic.

title: Restore agent loop as application-layer concern in docs
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: update the current architecture docs so the agent loop is explicitly named again, but framed as an application-layer concern within the accepted layered monolith design.

title: Clarify planned HTTP API in design docs
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: update the project docs so they state that the HTTP API is definitely planned from the beginning, while still not being the primary v0 entry point. The docs should make clear that the layering must enable the LiveView UI and planned HTTP API to share as much lower-level logic as possible.

title: Align docs with current LiveView-first design
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: update the main design docs so they reflect the actual current project state (fresh Phoenix + LiveView auth scaffold), remove confusion from the superseded API-first direction, make the first vertical slice the next discussion topic, and merge the two existing handoff docs into one canonical up-to-date handoff.

title: Task 7: Real auggie adapter, parser fixtures, and final slice verification
description:
Implements Task 7 from `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md`.

## Required Reading
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Task 7

## Scope
Replace the fake-only execution path with the real `auggie` adapter while preserving the shared backend contract.
- Capture representative `auggie` fixtures for model discovery and turn execution.
- Implement the real adapter using `System.cmd/3` for `auggie model list --json` and one-shot turn execution.
- Support deployment-level runtime configuration for CLI executable path, shared workspace root, and per-backend default model override.
- Persist backend-specific request/response snapshots, keep normalized event extraction modest if needed, and avoid brittle parsing.
- Run the final verification set for the slice.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Adapter tests prove model-list parsing and execution-result normalization for success/failure fixtures.
- [ ] The real adapter conforms to the previously implemented `list_models/0` and `run_turn/1` contracts.
- [ ] Focused adapter verification passes: `mix test test/ex_claw/assistant/backends/auggie_test.exs`
- [ ] Final slice verification passes: `mix test`
- [ ] Final slice verification passes: `mix precommit`
- [ ] Perform a lightweight browser sanity check covering login, `/` redirect to `/assistant`, `/assistant` redirect to `/assistant/sessions`, session management, message sending, and inline debug visibility.
- [ ] After completion, summarize changes and verification, ask the user to review/commit, and do not self-commit.

title: Task 6: Async runner, transcript updates, retry flow, and debug UI
description:
Implements Task 6 from `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md`.

## Required Reading
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Task 6

## Scope
Add the runtime execution flow and developer-friendly debug UI using the fake backend.
- Implement `ExClaw.Assistant.Runner` under `ExClaw.Assistant.TaskSupervisor` and broadcast session updates via PubSub.
- In `SessionsLive`, on send, persist the user message immediately, start a run through the runner, and disable the composer while the session has an active run.
- Keep archived sessions read-only with the message `This session is archived.`
- Re-stream transcript/session state on PubSub updates.
- Render success/failure states distinctly, support retry without duplicating the original user message, and show inline expandable debug detail per turn.
- Ensure request building uses the most recent prior non-null `backend_session_id`, even if that prior run failed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runner tests cover start, success, failure, retry semantics, and backend-session continuation lookup.
- [ ] LiveView tests cover immediate user-message persistence, active-run composer disabling, archived-session composer disabling, success/failure rendering, retry without duplicate user messages, and inline debug detail.
- [ ] Focused verification passes:
- [ ] `mix test test/ex_claw/assistant/runner_test.exs`
- [ ] `mix test test/ex_claw_web/live/sessions_live_test.exs`
- [ ] Perform a lightweight browser sanity check for send/retry/debug behavior.
- [ ] After completion, summarize changes and verification, ask the user to review/commit, and do not self-commit.

title: Phase 3: Run orchestration and real auggie integration
description:
Implements the execution/orchestration and real-backend portion of `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` (Tasks 6-7).

## Context
This phase adds the async runner, PubSub refresh flow, success/failure/retry UX, inline debug detail, and the real `auggie` adapter with final verification.

## Required Reading
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-design.md`
- `docs/plans/assistant-slice-1-implementation.md` Tasks 6-7

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runs execute through the application-level runner with correct lifecycle persistence.
- [ ] Transcript/debug UX reflects success, failure, retry, and archived/read-only rules.
- [ ] The real `auggie` adapter works through the shared backend contract and final verification passes.
